test.note: 1573 tests passed. One flaky test (double_buffer_read_consistency_race) failed on first run due to thread scheduling non-determinism, passed on retry and second full run.

lint.note: No separate lint command configured. Compiler warnings present (unused variables, dead code) but these are pre-existing and not introduced by pipeline-fixes features.

nonBlockingIssues.description: New-position damage path has same latent truncation pattern (i32→u16 cast) that was fixed in old-position path. At scale=2, physical coords could exceed u16::MAX for larger displays (not current 1024x768).
Missing invariant comment on negative-coordinate guard (ox >= 0 && oy >= 0) in compositor damage tracking.
Five parameters in update_document_content() are now unused (prefixed with _) after removing the 75% fallback path. Could be cleaned up from the function signature.
write_reader_done_gen casts &[u8] to *mut u32 (technical UB in Rust's memory model). Pragmatically correct for bare-metal shared memory where both sides use raw pointers, but would fail under Miri.
u32 generation wrap-around edge case: at u32::MAX, the 'back_gen > 0' guard would skip the reader-ack safety check. Theoretical only (~2.2 years at 60fps).

Code duplication between update_clock (line 327) and update_clock_inline (line 727) — ~30 lines of identical unsafe pointer arithmetic. Could be refactored to compose one over the other per the 'no parallel implementations' principle.

flakyTests.description: Multi-threaded test with 10,000 iterations failed on first run ('reader should have completed at least one read'), passed on retry. Thread scheduling non-determinism. The assertion expects at least one read completes within the test duration, but under test-thread serialization and system load, the reader thread may not get scheduled.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented reader acknowledgement protocol (finish_read/reader_done_gen) as step 5-6 of the double-buffer protocol. Documented PREV_BOUNDS damage tracking pattern with i32 type constraint for physical coordinates at scale≥2.
Documented QEMU disk image locking workaround: when user's QEMU holds a lock on test.img, workers must copy to /tmp/worker-test.img.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add 9p filesystem requirement to the QEMU visual test section. The manual QEMU launch command needs -fsdev and -device virtio-9p-device flags for font loading. Without them, the compositor reports 'no font data' and renders black. Workers should use test-qemu.sh when possible, or add the 9p flags when launching manually.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three consecutive workers (fix-full-rebuild-change-list, fix-data-buffer-compaction, fix-timer-input-coincidence) all reported getting black screens when following the manual QEMU launch instructions, then discovering the 9p requirement by reading test-qemu.sh.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the Architecture Notes to mention the reader acknowledgement step in the double-buffer protocol. Current text 'Scene graph is double-buffered in shared memory with generation counters (no locks)' should note that DoubleReader.finish_read() must be called after reading, and all core update methods check the return value of copy_front_to_back().
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-double-buffer-race reviewer noted the os-worker skill's Architecture Notes are stale after the reader acknowledgement protocol was added.

rejectedObservations.observation: The fix introduces update_clock_inline as a parallel implementation of update_clock — violates 'no parallel implementations' principle.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a minor refactoring observation, not an actionable guidance update. Both functions are in the same file, serve different calling contexts (standalone vs composable), and could be refactored at leisure. Not a systemic issue requiring guidance changes.

rejectedObservations.observation: write_reader_done_gen shared-memory cast pattern should be documented in safety-map.md
rejectedObservations.reason: The safety-map.md already covers the project's safety patterns comprehensively. This specific pattern is well-documented via its SAFETY comment in the source code. Adding it to safety-map.md would be redundant with the inline documentation and is a low-value addition.
